## [00:00 - 02:59]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Is Metta trying to put agencies out of business? Let's go. So during Metta's Q1 earnings call, Mark Zuckerberg raised some eyebrows when talking about his vision for the role of AI in advertising. Here's what he said and I quote, our goal is to make it so that any business can basically tell us what objective they're trying to achieve like selling something or getting a new customer and how much they're willing to pay for each result and Then we just do the rest end quote make no mistake He's taking direct aim at ad agencies The entire business model is at risk if his vision becomes reality Now not surprisingly a whole lot of people were angered by these comments I'm also seeing some who are laughing it off as being impossible, but you're not gonna hear me laugh this off I believe that it's probable that we end up somewhere close to what Zuckerberg describes That question will be the timeline now. Let's back up for a moment. What is he actually saying here? What are the obstacles to achieving this and what will it mean for advertisers in the ad agency business model? Let's take a closer look. So first allow me to rephrase What was said on that earnings call any business will be able to provide the following information an Objective an explanation of what they want like a sale. How much they're willing to pay for that result Now maybe he's abbreviating the process I'm gonna take his word for it that his vision doesn't require any other data points once you provide that info Metta will do the rest so everything Including the ad copy and creative I've also heard the theory of infinite ads Where meta can iterate countless variations to find what works now This sounds crazy and amazing and risky and scary all at the same time Now forget about the ad side of this for a moment and the obstacles that there may be We're basically there in this vision when it comes to sales campaigns Advanced plus shopping campaigns which rest in peace being replaced by advanced plus sales campaigns Eliminated all of the various Customizations that we were used to no targeting no manual placements one ad set just let the algorithm do its work So truthfully that part of campaign creation is already a reality Even if there are exceptions when customizations are necessary the big hurdle of course is the ads This is where meta loses me a bit because it's just so difficult to imagine

## [03:00 - 05:59]

So medic can generate the copy and creative for limitless ads with minimal input Now my assumption is that in this situation Metta has your pixel and customer data and knows a lot about your business already So in theory you may not even need to provide the links kind of like dynamic ads with a catalog But on steroids but those ads are all tightly structured based on the title description price and image in the catalog Right. That's how dynamic ads work when it comes to this vision We're missing a lot of details here But I assume that meta is gonna have to know your site and your business in order to generate everything But how does it do it? Well, that's what sounds crazy Think for a moment about how much we're already inviting AI to build our ads for us But with supervision so meta has AI generated text recommendations available now Of course, I find these be pretty bad at the moment and I never accept them But meta also rolled out a new branding feature that allows you to define the tone That can be used in AI generated text. So this is going to get better I know advertisers laugh at the possibility of automated everything Because there are so many issues with Metta's AI powered creative now But many of these same advertisers are adopting more AI to create their ads than ever before and I am one of them So they're using chat GPT and other tools to help brainstorm a marketing plan They're having AI write ad text for them or at least help them write that text And now they're even having AI produce the images and videos and in some cases the videos star AI Generated characters. So yes, we can't deny this We're already relying on AI more and more to create our ads and yes The tools available even Metta's tools are getting better But right now our input is still required What Mark Zuckerberg describes is a huge leap forward from where we are now It assumes you need to provide very little information at all and Metta will create your ads for you Maybe infinite ads until it finds what works best Worst case scenario, the ad copy will say something that's completely wrong You could also do major damage to your brand if you're not careful and that is what I believe is the biggest obstacle to achieving Zuckerberg's vision Brands have too much at risk for a campaign to be that simple Imagine a big brand like a Nike Pepsi Coca-Cola with multiple levels of legal and approval and red tape. This couldn't ever fly They would require approvals before going live, but maybe there's some middle ground here, right?

## [06:00 - 08:59]

Metta could generate all the ads with minimal input and you would need to approve each ad first before it's published And you could upload your brand standards in general directions Kind of like a system prompt and all the details that will be needed to craft the ads Now you could also argue that the fully automated campaign could be a great option for small businesses Maybe they don't have the money to hire an agency and maybe they don't have the time to learn all the best practices and features for creating a campaign I could see this being the future of the boost button, right? It may not be the perfect solution, but it could be better than something that your typical small business could do themselves Now that seems very realistic and really quite likely While I have my doubts about applying this to big brands with plenty to lose Nothing Mark Zuckerberg said on that earnings call is impossible Now it would be unrealistic to suggest it could happen this year in any meaningful way Because that's not going to happen, but five years from now We've seen how much everything has changed in about two and a half years with AI Would it be crazy to think that this could be possible in another two and a half years? So what does this mean for ad agencies in the digital advertising industry? Can't sugarcoat it. It could turn it upside down Now I know that the reaction is that this is impossible and advertisers are too important for every step of the campaign But I hear many of those same complaints when it comes to much of the automations that advertisers reject today They reject algorithmic targeting because they've tied their value to an ability to find the ideal audience But it's rarely necessary If you're going to tell me that I can create infinite ads with minimal effort or Maintenance and the results would be better than doing it manually. Yeah, sign me up But if that's possible, what does an ad agency do now? Clearly there's a long list of unanswered questions regarding what this is all going to look like But we've already known that you shouldn't build your agency's value on targeting and really you shouldn't build Much of what is done in the campaign and ad set right now But now the question is whether you should even focus on creating the ads I encourage you to set aside your defensiveness when thinking about this possibility and plan accordingly Yes, this could potentially end your business That's scary, but do not walk into this willfully ignorant about what is likely coming Be flexible while focusing on the things that have the most long-term value Ad creation and management does have value right now The positioning of the product and offer do have value the landing page experience has value
